questions.question: The Miller-Urey experiment demonstrated:

options.A: Formation of DNA from inorganic molecules
options.B: Synthesis of organic molecules (amino acids) from inorganic gases

questions.question: Darwin's theory of evolution is based on:

options.B: Natural selection acting on heritable variation

questions.question: Hardy-Weinberg principle states that allele frequencies remain constant if:

options.B: Large population with no mutation, migration, selection or non-random mating

questions.question: The fossil record provides evidence of evolution by showing:

options.B: Progressive changes in organisms over time in rock strata

questions.question: Homo sapiens evolved approximately:

options.B: 300,000 years ago in Africa

questions.question: Analogous organs are those that:

options.A: Have common ancestry but different functions
options.B: Have similar function but different evolutionary origin

questions.question: The diagram shows the geological time scale. In which era did dinosaurs flourish?
questions.diagram_description: Geological time scale diagram showing eons, eras, periods: Precambrian (4600–541 Mya, origin of life, prokaryotes, eukaryotes), Paleozoic (541–252 Mya, fish, amphibians, reptiles appear), Mesozoic (252–66 Mya – Triassic/Jurassic/Cretaceous, dinosaurs dominate), Cenozoic (66 Mya–present, mammals diversify, hominids, human evolution). Each era labeled with major events

options.B: Mesozoic (Triassic–Cretaceous, 252–66 Mya)

questions.question: Adaptive radiation is:

options.A: Migration of species to new areas
options.B: Diversification of one ancestral species into many species in different habitats

questions.question: The first hominid to use fire was:

options.B: Homo erectus

questions.question: Convergent evolution leads to:

options.B: Analogous organs (similar structures from different ancestry)

questions.question: The diagram shows Darwin's finches with different beak shapes. What type of evolution does this demonstrate?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of Galapagos finches showing multiple species with different beak shapes: Large ground finch (thick beak for crushing seeds), Cactus finch (curved beak for cactus), Warbler finch (thin beak for insects), Woodpecker finch (uses tools). All descended from common finch ancestor. Map of Galapagos Islands showing isolation of populations on different islands

options.B: Adaptive radiation (divergent evolution from one ancestor)

questions.question: Lamarck's theory of evolution proposed:

options.B: Inheritance of acquired characters (use and disuse)

questions.question: Which of the following is the OLDEST hominid fossil found?

options.B: Ardipithecus ramidus (4.4 Mya)

questions.question: Biochemical evidence for evolution includes:

options.B: Similarities in DNA, proteins (cytochrome c) across species

questions.question: Industrial melanism in peppered moths is an example of:

options.B: Natural selection (dark moths survived better on soot-darkened trees)

questions.question: The diagram shows comparative embryology across vertebrates. What conclusion does this evidence support?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram showing early embryo stages of fish, amphibian, reptile, bird, and human side by side at three developmental stages. Early stage: all show pharyngeal gill arches, similar curved body shape, tail present. Middle stage: divergence begins. Late stage: species-specific features emerge. Labels on each embryo type

options.B: Early embryos of different vertebrates are remarkably similar, showing common ancestry
options.C: Embryos develop at the same rate

questions.question: Speciation through geographic isolation is called:

options.B: Allopatric speciation

questions.question: The concept of 'survival of the fittest' was coined by:

options.B: Herbert Spencer (adopted by Darwin)

questions.question: Which hominid had a brain volume closest to modern humans?

options.B: Homo neanderthalensis (~1500 cc)

questions.question: Punctuated equilibrium theory suggests evolution occurs:

options.B: In rapid bursts followed by long periods of stasis

questions.question: The diagram shows three types of natural selection curves. Which type increases frequency of extreme phenotypes?
questions.diagram_description: Three graphs of population distribution before and after selection: (A) Stabilizing – bell curve narrows, extremes eliminated, mean preserved; (B) Directional – bell curve shifts to one side, one extreme favored; (C) Disruptive/Diversifying – bimodal distribution formed, both extremes favored, intermediate eliminated. Arrows showing selection pressure in each case

options.B: Directional selection

questions.question: Analogous organs prove which type of evolution?

options.B: Convergent evolution

questions.question: The 'Out of Africa' hypothesis states:

options.B: Modern humans originated in Africa and migrated to other continents
options.C: Humans evolved simultaneously in all continents
options.D: Neanderthals were direct ancestors of all humans

questions.question: Gene pool is defined as:

options.A: Pool of cells in a population
options.B: Total genetic information (all alleles) in all members of a breeding population
options.C: Only dominant alleles in a population

questions.question: Vestigial organs in humans include all EXCEPT:

options.B: Ear muscles

questions.question: The diagram shows Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium. If q² = 0.09 (frequency of affected recessive), what is carrier frequency (2pq)?
questions.diagram_description: Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium diagram: p + q = 1. Genotype frequencies: p²(AA) + 2pq(Aa) + q²(aa) = 1. Example: q² = 0.09, so q = 0.3, p = 0.7. Carrier frequency 2pq = 2 × 0.7 × 0.3 = 0.42 (42%). Pie chart showing proportion of each genotype in population. Graph of frequencies at equilibrium

questions.question: Mass extinction at end of Cretaceous period (66 Mya) eliminated:

options.B: ~75% of species including non-avian dinosaurs

questions.question: Primates evolved from which group?

options.B: Insectivore-like early mammals in late Cretaceous

questions.question: The first eukaryotic cells evolved approximately:

options.B: 1.5–2 billion years ago

questions.question: Genetic drift is most significant in:

options.B: Small isolated populations

questions.question: The diagram shows homologous forelimb structures of different vertebrates. What evolutionary principle does this support?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram comparing forelimb bones of: Human (arm bones – humerus, radius, ulna, carpals, metacarpals, phalanges for manipulation), Bat (same bones elongated for flight membrane), Whale (same bones compressed and paddle-shaped for swimming), Horse (same bones with fused and reduced digits for running). All labeled with same bone names showing homology

options.B: Divergent evolution from a common ancestor (homologous structures)

questions.question: Australopithecus was primarily:

options.B: Bipedal with small brain (~450 cc), lived 4–2 Mya in Africa
options.C: Had larger brain than Homo erectus

questions.question: Which gas was NOT present in the primitive Earth's atmosphere?

options.B: Free oxygen (O2)

questions.question: The endosymbiotic theory explains the origin of:

options.B: Mitochondria and chloroplasts from prokaryotic endosymbionts

questions.question: Coacervates are:

options.B: Colloidal droplets of proteins/lipids that may have been predecessors of cells

questions.question: The 'Cambrian explosion' refers to:

options.B: Rapid diversification of most animal phyla ~541 Mya in oceans

questions.question: Homo habilis is significant because:

options.A: Was the first to use fire
options.B: Was the first tool-using human (stone tools, ~2.5 Mya)
options.C: Was first to migrate out of Africa

questions.question: The diagram shows human evolution timeline. What is the correct sequence?
questions.diagram_description: Timeline of human evolution showing hominid species and approximate dates: Sahelanthropus tchadensis (7 Mya), Ardipithecus (4.4 Mya), Australopithecus afarensis/Lucy (3.2 Mya), Homo habilis (2.5 Mya, first tools), Homo erectus (1.9 Mya, fire, migrated to Asia), Homo heidelbergensis (0.7 Mya), Homo neanderthalensis (0.4–0.04 Mya, Europe), Homo sapiens (0.3 Mya, Africa). Silhouettes showing increasing upright posture and brain size

options.A: Homo erectus → Australopithecus → Homo habilis → Homo sapiens
options.B: Australopithecus → Homo habilis → Homo erectus → Homo sapiens

questions.question: Founder effect is a type of:

options.B: Genetic drift (small group colonizes new area with limited gene pool)

questions.question: Neanderthals (Homo neanderthalensis) are considered:

options.B: A separate species that interbred with Homo sapiens before extinction

questions.question: The 'molecular clock' concept assumes:

options.B: DNA mutations accumulate at roughly constant rate allowing evolutionary time estimates

questions.question: Biogeography supports evolution by showing:

options.B: Related species found in geographically connected regions (island vs mainland species)
options.C: All species originated at same place
options.D: No correlation between location and species

questions.question: Speciation is defined as:

options.B: Formation of new species that are reproductively isolated from ancestral species

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of co-evolution?

options.B: Flowers and their pollinators evolving together

questions.question: Homologous organs are evidence of:

options.B: Divergent evolution from common ancestor

questions.question: The diagram shows antibiotic resistance evolution in bacteria. Which mechanism best explains this?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram showing antibiotic resistance evolution: Large bacterial population with mostly sensitive bacteria (red) and rare resistant mutants (blue). Antibiotic applied – sensitive bacteria killed, resistant survive and reproduce. After several generations – population now mostly resistant. This demonstrates: pre-existing variation + selection pressure = evolution. NOT Lamarckian (bacteria did not acquire resistance in response to antibiotic)

options.A: Bacteria mutated in response to antibiotic (Lamarckian)
options.B: Pre-existing resistant mutants were selected by antibiotic (Darwinian natural selection)
options.D: Antibiotic caused DNA repair in bacteria

questions.question: Which era is called the 'Age of Reptiles'?

options.B: Mesozoic

questions.question: The bottleneck effect occurs when:

options.B: Population dramatically decreases, reducing genetic diversity

questions.question: First photosynthetic organisms were:

options.B: Cyanobacteria (blue-green algae, produced O2, ~2.7 Bya)

questions.question: Reproductive isolation between two populations leads to:

options.B: Speciation (formation of new species)
